name: Herdr

runner.notes: Prepare a run worktree, start herdr, open the worktree in a pane, launch the guest agent CLI with prompt.md, then run harness verification. Single-task runs measure the guest CLI more than Herdr itself; record environment=herdr plus the guest agent/model as separate run dimensions.

inclusion: Direct terminal-native agent runtime and multi-agent ADE competitor.
pricing_model: Open source (AGPL) single binary, no account or telemetry; sponsorship-funded with negotiable commercial licensing.
public_stack_signals: Rust single binary using ratatui, portable-pty (vendored patch), tokio, and interprocess; no Electron.
Server-client model over Unix domain sockets / Windows named pipes with newline-delimited JSON and a bundled JSON Schema (herdr api schema).
Per-agent detection manifests in TOML with a remote catalog at herdr.dev/agent-detection/index.toml, hot-updatable without a binary release.
Lifecycle hook installation into agent configs (claude hooks, codex/kimi config blocks, hermes plugin) for session identity and native resume.
Astro website with docs, plugin marketplace, and public stats; ~173k LOC Rust across 208 files at review time.
capabilities_to_probe: persistent PTY sessions with detach/reattach and SSH/phone thin-client attach
semantic agent state (idle/working/blocked/done) across ~20 runtime CLIs
agent-shaped socket API: pane read/send/split/run, wait output --match, wait agent-status
public agent skill (npx skills add) enabling agents to orchestrate sibling agents
git worktree create/open/remove with dirty-checkout safety
plugin system (herdr-plugin.toml) sharing the CLI/socket control surface
terminal session observe/control streams for third-party integrations
native agent session resume via installed lifecycle hooks
detection explainability (agent explain) and state-authority arbitration
moat_hypotheses: owned persistent PTY runtime with detach, SSH, direct-agent attach, and experimental live server handoff
broad explainable agent-state compatibility through foreground-process detection, lifecycle integrations, and hot-reloadable screen manifests
terminal-independent distribution as one Rust binary with a typed CLI/socket control surface
agent-operable workspace primitives spanning panes, waits, worktrees, terminal observe/control streams, and executable plugins
rapid open-source adoption and ecosystem momentum around a terminal-native agent runtime
setup_notes: Install via curl script or Homebrew; Windows install is beta (PowerShell script).

Dual-read tracked in intelligence: competitor today AND potential runtime backend (terminal session observe/control primitives could replace tmux-layer responsibilities); any backend spike is gated on AGPL/distribution decision, solo-maintainer bus factor, and the ungoverned same-user socket authority (no per-agent identity or capability scoping).

sources.notes: Positioning (one terminal, whole herd), install paths, comparison framing, no-account/no-telemetry claims.

Full source read at post-v0.7.3 HEAD (2026-07-09): detection manifests, agent_resume, worktree ops, integration hook installers, SKILL.md. Refresh 2026-07-15: ~16.8k stars, default branch master, still AGPL-marked, active same-day pushes; raw main/README 404s because default branch is master.
NDJSON request/response protocol, method families, events.subscribe, env injection, socket paths; no caller authentication beyond same-user file permissions.
Public SKILL.md teaching agents to inspect/control panes, spawn helper agents, and wait on state; HERDR_ENV=1 guard is agent-honor-system.
Self-positioning against tmux/Zellij, cmux/Warp, Solo, Conductor/Emdash/Superset; claims unique combo of PTY runtime + semantic state + agent-shaped API.
Homebrew formula confirms distribution channel and install count telemetry.

research.positioning: Agent multiplexer that lives in your terminal: tmux-style persistent PTY runtime extended with semantic agent state for ~20 coding CLIs, SSH/mobile attach, and an agent-shaped socket API that lets agents and plugins drive the workspace.

frontend: ratatui TUI inside the user's existing terminal
mouse-first panes/tabs/workspaces with responsive mobile switcher
backend: server-client over Unix sockets / named pipes, NDJSON protocol
per-agent TOML detection manifests with remote catalog updates
packaging: curl install script, Homebrew formula, Windows beta installer
no account, no telemetry, no Electron
data: session persistence and restore snapshots under ~/.config/herdr
in-memory 512-event ring for client sync (no durable audit log)
unknowns: Remote manifest catalog signing/provenance not evidenced in source at review time.
Commercial licensing terms and long-term funding model are not public.

infrastructure: Local-first single binary; server keeps PTYs alive across client detach, SSH reattach, and thin-client (herdr --remote) access.
Lifecycle hooks installed into agent configs provide session identity and native resume across ~20 runtime CLIs.
Terminal session observe/control streams expose read-only frames and single-owner input authority for third-party products.

agent_support: ~20 runtimes detected via manifests (claude, codex, gemini, cursor, devin, antigravity, cline, opencode, copilot, kimi, kiro, droid, amp, grok, hermes, kilo, qodercli, pi, omp, mastracode).
Any terminal agent runs unrecognized as a plain pane.
orchestration: Read/send/wait/split/spawn primitives via CLI+socket and a public agent skill.
No delegation contracts, caller identity, verification, or approval layer — coordination is ungoverned plumbing.
workspace_isolation: Workspace/tab/pane hierarchy plus git worktree create/open/remove with dirty-checkout safety.
Worktrees are layout convenience, not authority containers.
review_shipping: None provided — no tasks, review, verification evidence, or delivery pipeline; left to the guest agent and operator.
remote_mobile: SSH from phone/tablet with touch-optimized switcher.
Thin-client remote attach (herdr --remote) with clipboard bridging.
context_memory: Session/layout persistence and native agent session resume via installed hooks.
integrations: Plugin marketplace (herdr-plugin.toml: actions, event hooks, TUI panes, link handlers) sharing the same control surface.
Hook-based integration into agent config files.

install_surface: curl -fsSL https://herdr.dev/install.sh | sh (or brew install herdr)
Start herdr, open run worktree in a pane
Launch guest agent CLI with prompt.md
parity_risks: Single-agent task runs measure the selected coding CLI inside Herdr more than Herdr itself; treat them as environment-compatibility runs.
Comparative Herdr claims need multi-agent scenarios: intervention burden, state-detection accuracy, isolation, persistence/recovery across detach/SSH, and coordination glue required.
No built-in verification/evidence/delivery — 'delivery completeness' scores as not-provided rather than zero-quality.

hypotheses: PTY/terminal correctness across modern agent TUIs is expensive to reproduce and compounds as a moat.
The lifecycle-hook adapter matrix accumulates per-runtime compatibility knowledge faster than editor-bound competitors.
Remote-updatable detection manifests remove the release-per-CLI-change treadmill competitors suffer.
A cross-editor terminal runtime has a larger reachable surface than any editor-bound control plane.
Plugin/skill ecosystem on a single control surface may compound adoption.
evidence: Source-read: 18 detection manifests with versioned rules, region semantics, and remote catalog fetch (256KB cap, engine versioning).
GitHub API 2026-07-09: 14.7k stars, 861 forks in ~3.5 months; near-daily releases (0.7.2 on 07-07, 0.7.3 on 07-08); issues past #1149.
Official downloads/stats page: ~112.6k GitHub asset downloads, ~11.1k Homebrew installs, 70 stable+preview releases.
HN launch thread (48714802): 165 points / 110 comments; praise for scroll/mouse/SSH-mobile; notably zero security questions about the ungoverned socket.
unknowns: Whether the same-user socket gains per-agent identity/capability scoping (terminal observe/control split hints the team understands capability separation).
Whether it moves up-stack into durable tasks/MCP/verification — the watch signal for direct collision with governed-orchestration products.
Sustainability: founder-led direction with practical bus factor near one.
